summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two cases: ZC 2023-004, a request for preliminary development plan approval for a major modification to the Heritage Woods PUD to allow up to six single-family residential lots on an undeveloped 38-acre parcel, and ZC 2023-005, proposed text amendments to the township zoning resolution. Multiple Heritage Woods residents expressed concerns about water pressure, traffic, drainage, construction impacts, lack of HOA control over new lots, and potential agricultural/animal uses. Both cases were approved with staff recommendations forwarded to the Board of Trustees.
decisions: ZC 2023-004: Motion to approve staff recommendation for preliminary development plan for major modification to Heritage Woods PUD (up to 6 single-family lots on 38 acres) - Passed unanimously
ZC 2023-005: Motion to approve proposed text amendments to township zoning resolution as recommended by staff - Passed unanimously
Approval of April 4th meeting minutes - Passed (with two abstentions from Settle and Pegram)
action_items: Staff to research prior zoning code amendments from approximately 2020-2021 regarding accessory structure height restrictions to determine if changes were already made and need to be removed from current proposal
Staff to verify corner lot provisions and accessory structure percentage changes previously approved
Text amendments to be forwarded to Board of Trustees for May 23rd meeting
Heritage Woods HOA president David Williams invited to submit written comments/recommendations regarding desired PUD regulations for new lots to planning and zoning department
Applicant Rick Stumpf to prepare final development plan for Heritage Woods PUD modification including shared access drive details, setback requirements, and development standards
Staff to coordinate with public works department regarding water pressure concerns from Heritage Woods residents
Staff to look into future land use map update and comprehensive review of R1/R2/R3 lot size standards
key_amounts: 38 acres - size of undeveloped Heritage Woods PUD parcel
Up to 6 single-family lots proposed (likely 3 lots of 10+ acres each)
75 existing homes in Heritage Woods subdivision
104 acres originally purchased by developer Mike West
42 lots originally planned for phase three by Mike West
2,000 square feet - current maximum for accessory structures proposed to be removed
7% of lot - accessory structure coverage limit to remain
25 feet - current maximum height for accessory structures
30 feet - height requested by potential buyer for accessory structures
10% - threshold for minor administrative relief proposed in text amendments
75 feet - minimum setback from street right-of-way for front yard accessory structures in R1
notable_topics: Heritage Woods PUD major modification - preliminary development plan for 6 lots on 38 undeveloped acres
Resident concerns about water pressure in Heritage Woods dead-end street system
Traffic and construction concerns from Heritage Woods residents
HOA governance and control over new lots not part of existing HOA
Potential agricultural uses and animal keeping on new lots
Green belt/buffer zone disputes between existing residents and new property boundaries
Text amendments: administrative relief authority for zoning inspector
Text amendments: barns/storage structures as principal use in R1 without a house
Text amendments: removal of 2,000 sq ft maximum for accessory structures
Text amendments: accessory structures in front yard for R1 lots over 2 acres
Text amendments: updated flag lot dimensional requirements
Discussion of future comprehensive land use plan update and potential new zoning districts
Western Water vs. public water utility distinction and fire protection implications
Stalled Domino's zoning case - applicant possibly looking at another location
notes: Multiple Heritage Woods residents were clearly upset about the proposed development, citing promises made by original developer Mike West that the 38-acre area would remain green space. The original developer had planned 42 lots for phase three but never built them, reportedly due to water pressure and access issues. The property owner Rick Stumpf noted he has received calls from developers who would want to build far more homes if given the opportunity. There was confusion among residents about what had been promised regarding green belts and buffer zones. The Zoning Commission discussed that prior code amendments from approximately 2020-2021 regarding accessory structure heights may not have been properly updated in the current zoning code, requiring staff research. The Domino's zoning case from a previous meeting appears to be stalled or dead.
title: ZC 5-2-23
